Name: tensor_core_adaptive_kv_cache
Title: TensorCoreKV: Adaptive Low-Bit KV-Cache Decoding via Hardware-Aware Spectral Precision Routing
Short Hypothesis: Dynamic precision allocation for KV-cache decoding, guided by profiled activation geometry and optimized for Tensor Core utilization, can achieve superior latency-accuracy tradeoffs compared to static precision schemes.
Related Work: BitDecoding (Du et al., 2025) optimizes KV-cache layouts for Tensor Cores but uses static precision. Ecosystem proposals explore spectral precision routing but lack hardware-faithful evaluation. Our work distinguishes by combining hardware-aware kernel optimization with dynamic precision allocation, validated through profiled benchmarks.
Abstract: Efficient KV-cache decoding is critical for long-context LLM inference, but static precision schemes fail to adapt to varying activation geometry, leaving performance untapped. We propose TensorCoreKV, a hardware-aware framework that dynamically allocates precision for KV-cache decoding based on profiled activation geometry. By extracting local covariance spectra during inference and mapping spectral decay rates to precision-switching policies, TensorCoreKV optimizes Tensor Core utilization while preserving accuracy. We introduce novel CUDA-Triton kernels that fuse precision adaptation with dequantization, enabling seamless transitions between bit-widths. Evaluated on Blackwell and Hopper GPUs, TensorCoreKV achieves a 6.8× decoding speedup over FP16 FlashDecoding-v2, with a 1.2× improvement over BitDecoding, while maintaining accuracy. Our profiler-driven validation isolates gains to specific hardware causes—improved tensor-core occupancy, reduced bank conflicts, and coalesced loads—and provides reproducible benchmarks for deployment.
Experiments: Compare decoding latency and accuracy of TensorCoreKV against BitDecoding and FP16 FlashDecoding-v2 on LLaMA-3.1-8B with 128K context, using Blackwell and Hopper GPUs.
Profile tensor-core occupancy, achieved bandwidth, and warp stall reasons for TensorCoreKV kernels, attributing gains to specific hardware optimizations.
Conduct ablation studies on precision adaptation policies, measuring impact on latency-accuracy tradeoffs.
Evaluate variance across runs with warmup iterations, locked clocks, and multiple GPUs/driver versions.
Risk Factors and Limitations: Dynamic precision adaptation may introduce overhead if spectral extraction outweighs decoding gains.
Optimizations may be architecture-specific, limiting generality across GPU generations.
Accuracy-preserving spectral thresholds may require per-model calibration.
